item.title: 3 killed in road accidents
item.description: At least three people were killed in road accidents in Rangpur and Noakhali on Thursday......

item.title: Malaysia agrees to reopen labour market for Bangladeshis
item.description: Malaysia has agreed to reopen its labour market for Bangladeshi workers and reduce migration costs......Bangladesh travel

item.title: Israel PM asks cabinet ‘to begin direct negotiations’ with Lebanon
item.description: Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Thursday he ordered his cabinet to open direct talks with Lebanon to disarm Hezbollah and establish ‘peace relations’ between the two countries......

item.title: BNP wins in Bogura 6, Sherpur 3
item.description: BNP candidates secured landslide victories in the Bogura-6 by-election and the Sherpur-3 polls held on Thursday, while Jamaat-e-Islami alleged widespread irregularities, including assaults on polling agents, centre occupation, ballot manipulation, and attempts to falsify results......

item.title: India election roll overhaul sparks anger as key states vote
item.description: A key opposition Indian politician has threatened legal action against the election commission after millions of people were struck off electoral rolls, the latest flashpoint over a contentious voter clean-up drive......

item.title: Six more die with measles symptoms
item.description: Six more children died with measles symptoms and 1,187 others were infected across Bangladesh in the 24 hours until 8:00am on Thursday......

item.title: Calls for US-Iran truce to extend to Lebanon after Israeli strikes
item.description: Calls were mounting on Thursday for the ceasefire between the US and Iran to be extended to Israel’s war with Hezbollah, after a massive wave of Israeli strikes on Lebanon killed more than 200 people......

item.title: Pakistan set to host US-Iran peace talks
item.description: Pakistan praised ‘restraint’ in the Middle East war on Thursday, but condemned on-going Israeli attacks on Lebanon as Islamabad prepared to host expected talks between Iran and the United States......

item.title: Trade deficit widens to $16.9b in July-Feb
item.description: Bangladesh’s trade deficit widened sharply in the first eight months of the current fiscal year as higher import payments and declining export earnings increased pressure on the external sector, according to Bangladesh Bank data......Bangladesh travel

item.title: Gulf war to eat up 1.2pc of GDP: SANEM
item.description: South Asian Network on Economic Modeling on Thursday said that Bangladesh’s economy might fall by 1.2 per cent because of price hikes of energy items in the wake of the war in the Gulf......

item.title: Renewable energy training facility inaugurated
item.description: Bangladesh Power Management Institute on Thursday inaugurated Renewable Energy Training Facility at its Purbachal Campus aiming at helping the country’s transition to the sustainable and climate-resilient energy future......

item.title: CHT products fair begins
item.description: A three-day fair celebrating the traditional festivals of ethnic communities of the Chittagong Hill Tracts began on Thursday at the Shakyamuni Buddhist Monastery premises at Mirpur in Dhaka......

item.title: 3-day int’l tourism fair begins
item.description: A three-day international tourism fair, titled ‘Dhaka Travel Mart 2026,’ began on Thursday in the capital, bringing together over 50 organisations to showcase travel, aviation and hospitality offerings......

item.title: Energy crisis, inflation strain economy: GED
item.description: The General Economics Division of the Planning Ministry has released its March 2026 economic update, highlighting mounting challenges from energy crisis and persistent inflation, despite some improvement in foreign exchange reserves......Maps

item.title: BB extends export facilitation fund tenure to 2030
item.description: In a significant move to bolster the country’s export earnings and ensure a steady flow of foreign currency, the Bangladesh Bank on Thursday extended the tenure of the Export Facilitation Pre-finance Fund until December 31, 2030......

item.title: Oil prices jump back toward $100
item.description: Oil prices jumped and most stock markets fell Thursday as investors weighed the prospects of a shaky Middle East ceasefire and a re-opening of the Strait of Hormuz, crucial to restoring oil and gas shipments......

item.title: Iran’s economy was weak before war, now it’s worse
item.description: Even before the war in sanctions-hit Iran, inflation was running at nearly 50 per cent and anger over the economy had fuelled massive anti-government protests. After more than five weeks of conflict, problems have only mounted......

item.title: US Fed policymakers flag possible rate hikes to tackle inflation
item.description: Many US central bank policymakers cited the possible need for interest rate hikes to counter the risk of sustained inflation from high oil prices, minutes of their recent meeting showed Wednesday......

item.title: Latest Anthropic AI model finds cracks in software defences
item.description: Anthropic on Tuesday said its yet-to-be-released artificial intelligence model called Claude Mythos has proven keenly adept at exposing software weaknesses......

item.title: Energy conservation, renewables stressed to tackle crisis
item.description: At a roundtable on Thursday, participants called for strengthening energy conservation and boosting use of renewable energy to tackle present and future energy crisis....

item.title: Sonali Bank arranges training workshop in Barishal
item.description: Sonali Bank PLC on Thursday arranged a training workshop on audit objection, settlement process and money loan cases it’s General Managers’ office in Barishal, said a press release......

item.title: Trump, Hegseth and the language of war crimes
item.description: HE IS out of ideas, a mind running on empty. Increasingly, he is also short of reason, zapped by geopolitical addling and meddling. Now that US President Donald J Trump has reached an uneasy understanding with Teheran that a two-week ceasefire should apply to the warring parties (Israel, as usual, has its own elastic interpretation as it continues attacking Lebanon)......

item.title: Strengthening community pharmacy
item.description: BUILDING an effective and people-oriented healthcare system in Bangladesh requires urgent attention to the community pharmacy sector, which remains one of the most accessible yet under-regulated components of healthcare delivery. While the government has taken steps to introduce hospital and clinical pharmacy services, the role of qualified pharmacists at the......Politics analysis

item.title: Dissolution exposes legitimacy gap
item.description: THE National Sports Council on April 7 dissolved the Bangladesh Cricket Board led by Aminul Islam Bulbul and put in place an 11-member ad hoc committee with former captain Tamim Iqbal at the helm. It happened after an NSC investigation of alleged irregularities on BCB elections held in October......

item.title: From fuel shock to food crisis
item.description: THE ongoing Iran war, now marked by a fragile 14-day ceasefire with limited passage reportedly allowed through the Strait of Hormuz, is rapidly emerging as one of the most severe global energy disruptions in recent years, with consequences that extend far beyond the immediate theatre of conflict. For Bangladesh, a country structurally dependent on imported......

item.title: The winner of Trump’s Iran war? Iran
item.description: TRUMP announced a two-week ceasefire on Tuesday, April 7, the 39th day of the Israeli-US war on Iran. He depended on Pakistani mediators and a 10-point peace plan put forward by Iran itself......

item.title: Disorderly bus parking keeps harming mobility
item.description: THE unchecked parking of inter-district buses along major roads and the proliferation of illegal roadside counters where passengers board outside designated terminals are alarming. Passengers and road users in the capital continue to face severe disruption as buses occupy large portions of road stretches near the three major inter-district terminals at Gabtali......

item.title: Govt must plan, invest in strategic energy reserve
item.description: VULNERABILITY to external energy shocks has once again come into sharp focus amid the ongoing Israeli-US war on Iran and its fallout on the Middle East. The country’s heavy reliance on imported fossil fuels, particularly from the geopolitically sensitive Middle East, leaves it exposed to sudden supply disruptions and price volatility. Although the recent ceasefire between the......

item.title: NU mulls introducing technical courses for bachelor’s degree
item.description: The authorities of National University are considering the inclusion of technical courses in its four-year bachelor’s programmes to help prepare students as a skilled workforce......

item.title: Crackdown on hill cutting ordered
item.description: State minister for environment, forest and climate change, Sheikh Faridul Islam, on Thursday ordered a crackdown on hill cutting, particularly in Chattogram and Sylhet regions and directed the formation of a probe committee to investigate reports of large-scale hill cutting in Khagrachhari district......

item.title: 133 sectarian violence incidents in 3 months: minorities’ council
item.description: The Bangladesh Hindu Buddhist Christian Unity Council on Thursday expressed deep concern over the continuation of communal violence across Bangladesh in the three months leading up to the 13th parliamentary elections......

item.title: Two students shot during armed groups’ clash at Chashara
item.description: Two innocent bystanders suffered bullet injuries during a clash between two armed groups in Narayanganj’s Chashara area on Thursday......

item.title: Hadi killing prime suspect Foysal gets 10yrs’ jail in arms case
item.description: A Dhaka court on Thursday sentenced Foysal Karim Masud, prime accused in the case lodged over the murder of Inquilab Mancha spokesperson Sharif Osman Hadi, to 10-year imprisonment in a case lodged under Arms Act......Geographic Reference

item.title: SUST agri, mineral sciences faculty confers dean’s awards
item.description: Agriculture and mineral sciences faculty of the Shahjalal University of Science and Technology on Thursday honoured two faculty members with Dean’s Awards for their special contributions to research in the university......

item.title: Professor Dalem Chandra trust fund established at DU
item.description: A new trust fund has been established at the University of Dhaka in memory of the founding chairperson of the DU peace and conflict studies department, Professor Dalem Chandra Barman......

item.title: Coast Guard holds meeting to strengthen security in Sundarban
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard held a coordination meeting at its base Mongla in Bagerhat on Thursday to strengthen the overall security of the country’s southwestern coastal waterways and the Sundarban......

item.title: 2 killed, 20 injured in two separate clashes at Derai in Sunamganj
item.description: Two people were killed and at least 20 others were injured in two separate clashes at Derai in Sunamganj on Wednesday evening......

item.description: The sociology department of Jagannath University held a seminar titled Career Planning and Gender Awareness at the JnU in Dhaka on Wednesday......Geographic Reference

item.title: Improving quality of services to students highest priority: BOU VC
item.description: Vice-chancellor of the Bangladesh Open University Professor Mohammad Siddiqur Rahman Khan on Wednesday said that improving the quality of services to students was his highest priority......

item.title: Govt to implement media reforms soon: Swapon
item.description: Information and broadcasting minister Zahir Uddin Swapon on Thursday said that the government would initiate a move soon to implement media reforms with the aim of turning its promises into concrete actions......

item.title: Case filed 7 years after Kushtia BNP leader’s death, ex-MP, DC, SP accused
item.description: A CASE was filed seven years after the death of a BNP leader in Kushtia, accusing a former lawmaker, a deputy commissioner and a superintendent of police, among others......

item.title: Anti-imperialism and War Alliance launched
item.description: Left leaningpoliticalparties on Thursday launched a new alliance, named the ‘Anti-imperialism and War Alliance,’ to continue their movements against imperialism and war in the world, especially against the Iran war and the ‘unequal’ trade deal signed with the United States government during the rule of the interim government in Bangladesh......Bangladesh travel

item.title: Youth crushed under train in Gazipur
item.description: An unidentified youth was killed after being run over by a train in Bou Bazar area of Tongi East police station early Thursday......

item.description: A three-day international conference on physics began at the Teachers-Students Centre auditorium of Dhaka University on Thursday......

item.title: ACC probes into alleged graft over JS sound system
item.description: The Anti-Corruption Commission has launched an inquiry into allegations of embezzlement of a huge amount of money and laundering abroad in the name of operating, repairing, and maintaining the sound system installed in the plenary hall of the Jatiya Sangsad Bhaban......

item.title: Jamaat-led alliance warns tougher movement if referendum verdict not implemented
item.description: Leaders of the Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami-led alliance on Thursday at a protest rally in the capital Dhaka said that they would wage tougher movements to compel the ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party to implement the referendum verdict given by the people on February 12......

item.title: Ex-DGFI chief Mamun put on fresh remand
item.description: An additional metropolitan magistrate court in Dhaka on Thursday placed former director general of the Directorate General of Forces Intelligence, Sheikh Mamun Khaled, on a fresh three-day remand in a case filed over the 2022 killing of Bangladesh Nationalist Party activist Mokbul Hossain......

item.title: BSF returns body of slain Bangladeshi after 17 hours
item.description: The Indian Border Security Force has returned the body of a Bangladeshi man who was killed in firing by the BSF members at the Dhabolguri border in Patgram upazila of Lalmonirhat......

item.title: ISPR warns of fraud using fake army, DGFI identities
item.description: The Inter-Services Public Relations directorate on Thursday urged the public to remain alert against fraudulent activities carried out using fake identities of Bangladesh Army and Directorate General of Forces Intelligence officials......

item.title: Ukraine lets firms deploy air defences
item.description: Facing daily barrages of Russian drones and missiles, Ukraine has invited private companies to get their own air-defence systems in a bid to ease the burden on its military......Hospital news

item.title: JS passes local govt bills amid walkout
item.description: Jatiya Sangsad on Thursday passed local government bills amid walkout staged by the opposition lawmakers......

item.title: Tight security measures for Pahela Baishakh celebrations
item.description: The government has taken tight security measures across the country to ensure the peaceful and smooth celebration of Bangla New Year 1433, as well as the traditional festivals of the Chakma, Marma, Tripura, Garo and other ethnic communities on April 14......

item.title: 43,044 litres of edible oil seized in Jashore
item.description: A mobile court in Jashore district town seized a large quantity of illegally stockpiled edible oil and fined the owner of a local warehouse for attempting to manipulate market supply......

item.title: App introduced to check hoarding of fuel oil
item.description: The government on Thursday introduced an application to check hoarding amid import disruptions of petroleum products from the war-hit Gulf region that forced consumers to struggle to collect fuel oils over the past one month......
